channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Strong earthquake in Taiwan injures 27 and causes scattered damage
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — A 6.4-magnitude earthquake struck southern Taiwan early Tuesday, leaving 27 people with minor injuries and some reported damage. The quake hit at 12:17 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Democrat congressman condemns 'what appears to be a Heil Hitler salute' from Musk
item.description: Rep. Jerry Nadler (D-N.Y.) was among those who sharply criticized billionaire Elon Musk on Monday for making "what appears to be a Heil Hitler salute" during an event celebrating the inauguration of President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, ABC 4

item.title: Trump rolls out his blueprint on border security, but his orders will face challenges
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump rolled out a blueprint to beef up security at the southern border in a series of executive orders that began taking effect soon after his inauguration Monday, making good on his defining political promise to crack down on immigration and marking another wild
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, GISELA SALOMON and ELLIOT SPAGAT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Rubio unanimously confirmed as secretary of State, becoming Trump's first Cabinet official
item.description: Rubio’s confirmation vote passed with overwhelming bipartisan support, 99-0. Rubio also was able to cast a vote for himself.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, ABC 4

item.title: Residents evacuated as brush fire threatens homes near San Diego
item.description: Several residents in a San Diego area community are being evacuated as firefighters work to battle a wind-driven brush fire that is threatening homes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senate passes Laken Riley Act in first move after Trump inauguration
item.description: The Senate on Monday passed the Laken Riley Act, making the immigration-related bill the first piece of legislation to make it through the upper chamber in the new Congress and putting it a step closer to being signed into law by President Trump. Senators voted 64-35 on the bill.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Planters hiring latest class of 'Nutmobile' drivers
item.description: Mr. Peanut is hiring. Planters is ready to bring in the 2025 class of brand ambassadors and drivers of their 26-foot peanut on wheels, the Nutmobile.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump to issue 10 executive orders cracking down on border
item.description: President-elect Trump is set to sign off on 10 executive orders Monday, moving swiftly to crack down on immigration and drug cartels during his first day in office with a blitzkrieg of immigration actions.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Biden pardons Fauci, Jan. 6 committee ahead of Trump inauguration
item.description: (The Hill) -- President Joe Biden granted pardons to several prominent public servants Monday who have faced attacks from President-elect Donald Trump in one of his final acts in office. Biden issued pardons for Dr.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Trump inauguration to kick off 100-day rush
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is set to be sworn in at the Capitol on Monday, solidifying his stunning return to the White House and kicking off what is expected to be a busy 100-day rush focused on a handful of hot-button issues.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, KRON4

item.title: Teen pleads guilty in fatal stabbings at Taylor Swift-themed dance class in England
item.description: LONDON (AP) — A teen pleaded guilty Monday to charges of killing three girls and wounding 10 other people in a stabbing rampage at a Taylor Swift-themed dance class in England last summer. Axel Rudakubana, 18, entered the surprise plea at the start of his trial at Liverpool Crown Court.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: TikTok restores service for US users based on Trump's promised executive order
item.description: TikTok restored service to users in the United States on Sunday just hours after the popular video-sharing platform went dark in response to a federal ban, which President-elect Donald Trump said he would try to pause by executive order on his first day in office.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Trump plans barrage of Day 1 executive actions on border, energy, federal workforce
item.description: President-elect Trump is planning a barrage of executive actions on energy, the border and immigration enforcement on his first day in office, four sources told The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WGN 9

item.title: Man admits to setting congressman's district office on fire over TikTok ban: police
item.description: Police said a Wisconsin man admitted to setting a congressman's district office on fire after the popular social media app TikTok went dark in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump returns to Washington for celebrations ahead of inauguration
item.description: Donald Trump returned to Washington Saturday evening to celebrate with family, supporters and political allies ahead of his second inauguration.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Bird flu found in a Georgia commercial flock for the 1st time amid the nationwide outbreak
item.description: For the first time since the 2022 countrywide outbreak, bird flu hit a poultry producer in Georgia, the nation's top state for chicken production.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Keke Palmer comedy ‘One of Them Days’ and ‘Mufasa’ in close race for No. 1
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Keke Palmer buddy comedy “One of Them Days” opened in first place on the North American box office charts on a particularly slow Martin Luther King Jr. holiday weekend.
item.dc:creator: LINDSEY BAHR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: These notable inaugurations weren't held at the US Capitol. Here's why
item.description: For decades, Americans have gathered at the Capitol Building in Washington D.C. to watch the inauguration of the incoming president, with some noteworthy exceptions.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: Pennsylvania man dies after setting himself, family on fire in attempted homicide
item.description: A man is deceased, and several members of his family were hospitalized, after he reportedly poured gasoline on himself, his family's bedrooms, and the rest of his house and set a fire in an attempted homicide in Pennsylvania.
item.dc:creator: Hayden Sherry, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Gaza ceasefire takes hold and the first handover of hostages is underway
item.description: A ceasefire between Israel and Hamas took hold in the Gaza Strip on Sunday, ushering in an initial six-week period of calm and raising hopes for the release of dozens of militant-held hostages and an end to the devastating 15-month war.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump inauguration on alert for lone wolf attacks: how to spot a bad actor
item.description: An expert weighed in on what law enforcement keep an eye on to spot potential lone wolf attackers during the Inauguration festivities for President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Need to get a tattoo removed? What to know about the risks, the cost and more
item.description: When you’re young and carefree, a spur-of-the-moment tattoo feels like the ultimate form of self-expression. Years later, though, you might have a change of heart.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, ABC 4

item.title: Most voters in new survey seek toned-down Trump agenda
item.description: Over 60% of Americans polled disagree with Trump's idea to replace thousands of career civil-service workers.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KRON4

item.title: Chicago prepares for deportation arrests targeting hundreds next week after Trump takes office
item.description: Federal immigration officers will target more than 300 people with histories of egregious, violent crimes after President-elect Donald Trump takes office Monday, an official said, marking his administration's initial attempt toward fulfilling his promise of large-scale deportations.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND and ELLIOT SPAGAT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A$AP Rocky’s shooting trial is set to begin. Here’s what to know about the case
item.description: A$AP Rocky is at a crossroads. He could soar to new heights of celebrity in 2025, or be brought back to earth quickly by a trial starting Tuesday that could lead to his imprisonment for years.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DALTON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Georgia appeals court sides with Trump in affirming dismissal of 6 state charges
item.description: A Georgia appeals court on Friday affirmed the dismissal of six counts in the state's 2020 election interference against President-elect Trump and his allies, upholding a trial judge's decision that the charges must be thrown out due to a lack of detail.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WGN 9

item.title: Navajo Nation firefighters battle wildfires in Los Angeles
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Firefighters from the Navajo Nation worked tirelessly through a haze of dust to cut away dirt from a narrow road at the side of a mountain struck by a landslide in Southern California, coughing and sneezing amid the backbreaking work.
item.dc:creator: JOHN LOCHER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Smoke from fire at California lithium battery plant raises concerns about air quality
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — A fire at the world’s largest battery storage plant in Northern California smoldered Friday after sending plumes of toxic smoke into the atmosphere, leading to the evacuation of up to 1,500 people. The blaze also shook up the young battery storage industry.
item.dc:creator: OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ and ISABELLA O'MALLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Kevin O'Leary says he's offering $20B cash for TikTok
item.description: Investor Kevin O’Leary, widely known as a star from “Shark Tank,” said he offered TikTok’s owners $20 billion in cash to buy the platform during a Friday appearance on Fox News’s “America’s Newsroom.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Homes were burning and roads already jammed when Pacific Palisades evacuation order came, AP finds
item.description: The first evacuation order covering neighborhoods closest to the start of the devastating Pacific Palisades wildfire didn’t come until about 40 minutes after some of those homes were already burning, according to an Associated Press analysis of emergency communications and interviews with survivors.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, GENE JOHNSON, CHRISTOPHER L. KELLER, CLAUDIA LAUER and AMY TAXIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Hollywood's awards season dates, and how they've been affected by the wildfires
item.description: Hollywood's awards season has all but come to a rare halt as the wildfires continue to disrupt life and work in the Los Angeles area. There are near daily updates from the Hollywood guilds and organizations that put on awards shows as the industry navigates the ongoing crisis.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Busta Rhymes turns himself in after fight with man in NYC, police say
item.description: Trevor Tanhiem Smith, Jr., 52, also known as Busta Rhymes, is accused of punching a man in the face during a fight last week, police said.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, Fox 5

item.title: Fires scorched campuses across Los Angeles. Many schools are seeking places to hold classes
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Days after losing her home in the same fire that destroyed her Los Angeles elementary school, third-grader Gabriela Chevez-Muñoz resumed classes this week at another campus temporarily hosting children from her school.
item.dc:creator: KRYSTA FAURIA, JOCELYN GECKER and CLAIRE RUSH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: White House says TikTok ban will fall to Trump administration
item.description: The White House said Friday that the TikTok ban will fall to President-elect Trump's administration after the Supreme Court upheld a law requiring the app's China-based parent company to divest from it.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Biden won't enforce TikTok ban, official says, leaving fate of app to Trump
item.description: President Joe Biden won't enforce a ban on the social media app TikTok that is set to take effect a day before he leaves office on Monday, a U.S. official said Thursday, leaving its fate in the hands of President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Pepsi discontinues 'permanent' flavor, debuts a completely different one
item.description: Pepsi is planning to debut a brand-new flavor this month, marking its first “permanent” addition to the brand’s beverage portfolio in four years.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, ABC 4

item.title: Capital One technical issue delays payments for users: What to know
item.description: Some Capital One users were still waiting to receive deposits and payments on Friday morning after the company experienced a processing disruption.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Jack, WGN 9
